steps.title: Recall the complementary angle property
steps.content: In a right triangle, the sum of all angles is $180^{\circ}$. With one right angle ($90^{\circ}$), the two acute angles must sum to $180^{\circ} - 90^{\circ} = 90^{\circ}$. They are complementary.

steps.title: Calculate the other acute angle
steps.content:
If one acute angle measures $51^{\circ}$, then the other acute angle is:
$$90^{\circ} - 51^{\circ} = 39^{\circ}$$
B is correct.
